title: Metastelma dorrii
taxonomy: Metastelma dorrii is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This classification suggests that Metastelma dorrii shares similarities with other plants in the Apocynaceae family. The genus Metastelma, to which Metastelma dorrii belongs, is also part of the Apocynaceae family. Metastelma dorrii is typically classified as a perennial vine, and its classification within the Apocynaceae family indicates its adaptations and growth patterns. The plant's classification also helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family.

intro: Metastelma dorrii, commonly known as Dorri's metaphyl or Mexican snake flower, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its twining stems and showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of M. dorrii include alkaloids, such as dorriane and dorrimine, as well as flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, M. dorrii is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health complaints.
